introduction:
The Uttar Pradesh Transport Department manages driving permits collectively via the central Sarathi portal. Securing a licence requires getting a Learner's Licence (LL) first, then a Permanent Driving Licence (DL).

To reduce in-person crowding at RTOs, UP strongly encourages using their Aadhaar-based Faceless system for Learner’s Licences.
quickFacts:
Crucial Path
  • LL Application: Test is online. No RTO visit needed if Aadhaar is linked with mobile.
  • DL Application: Apply exactly 30 days post-LL issuance.
  • Test Track: Physical skill test on tracks at UP RTO is mandatory.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

For Learner's Licence (LL)
  • Aadhaar Card (For E-KYC signature)
  • Age Proof Document
  • Current Address Proof
  • Digital Passport format photo
For Permanent DL
  • Valid Learner's Licence printout
  • Application Form 4 & Fee Receipt
  • Vehicle Documents (RC book, Insurance, PUC)
  • Medical declaration form (Form 1A if Transport)
onlineApplication:
How to Apply Online

Stage 1: Acquire your e-Learner's Licence
  1. Go to sarathi.parivahan.gov.in and select Uttar Pradesh.
  2. Choose "Apply for Learner Licence" and opt for Authentication via Aadhaar.
  3. Fill up the application, upload documents and signatures, and pay the test fees.
  4. Complete the informative Road Safety video tutorial.
  5. Take the LL Computer Test from the comfort of your home. If passed, download your digital Learner's Licence immediately!
Stage 2: Acquire your Permanent DL
  1. Wait exactly 30 days. Go back to Sarathi and click "Apply for Driving Licence".
  2. Upload your LL details and remit the DL Test and Smart Card charges (approx ₹1000).
  3. Book your Driving Skill Test appointment at a suitable Uttar Pradesh RTO office.
offlineApplication:
How to Apply Offline / Driving Test

The practical assessment must be done at the scheduled UP RTO:

  1. Carry all original documents and your test vehicle displaying the required 'L' board sticker.
  2. Present yourself to the Motor Vehicle Inspector (MVI).
  3. You will have to maneuver successfully through designated track shapes including '8' or 'H' based on the vehicle category applied for.
afterApplying: Once Approved: Details are instantly sent to the centralized database. You can digitally access the DL via DigiLocker, while the PVC Smart Card Driving Licence gets routed via Indian Post to your verifiable address.
helpfulLinks: MoRTH Sarathi Portal UP Transport Dept
